title: DOM State, Events, and Accessible Interaction · Independent Script Trial
summary: Build a keyboard-operable service-status interface whose DOM, focus, announcements, and visual state stay synchronized. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of an accessible interactive service-status interface with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Query, create, move, replace, and remove DOM nodes with explicit missing-element and ownership handling.
Derive DOM output from application state without duplicating conflicting sources of truth.
Trace event target, currentTarget, capture, bubble, composed paths, and cancellation before delegating behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
lab case — An accessible status filter has 5 options, works by keyboard and pointer, survives dynamic list updates through delegation, restores focus after removal, and announces the changed result count.
